page_content:
Office of Diversity and Inclusion

The Counseling Center

Shoemaker Building 301-314-7651 counseling.umd.edu

Many students encounter a variety of personal, social, career, and academic issues that call for assistance beyond advice provided by friends and family. The Counseling Center provides free and confidential services by professional counselors to all University of Maryland students. The two divisions of the Counseling Center are the Counseling Service and the Research Unit.

The Counseling Service

301-405-2838 diversity.umd.edu

The Office of Diversity and Inclusion provides leadership and expertise to support the university achieve its diversity, equity and inclusion goals. ODI houses several units that offer support to students, including ADA compliance, Bias Incident Support Services (BISS), the LGBTQ+ Equity Center, the Nyumburu Cultural Center, the Office of Multi-ethnic Student Education (OMSE) and TerrapinSTRONG Education. ODI also coordinates with Diversity Officers and other partners across campus. Students may contact ODI at DiverseTerps@umd.edu for support

301-314-7651 counseling.umd.edu/cs

or to be connected to resources.

The Counseling Service, comprised of licencsed professional counselors, psychologists, and social workers, provides time-limited individual, group and couples psychotherapy counseling for a variety of psychological, interpersonal, and career issues.
